name: Brazos Bend 50
description: Trail Racing Over Texas's flagship April event at Brazos Bend State Park, offering distances from 5K to 50 miles on one of the flattest trail courses in Texas. The 50K serves as the 2026 RRCA Texas State Championships. All races start and finish at Elm Lake Pavilion among the park's famous alligators and wildlife.

name: Bellaire Trolley Run
description: The 30th Annual Bellaire Trolley Run starts in front of Bellaire City Hall with a 1-mile division at 7:30 AM and the 5K at 8:00 AM, plus a wheelchair 5K and stroller 5K. A beloved community tradition, all finishers receive a medal and t-shirt, followed by a post-race party.

name: Spring Fling - Houston
description: A multi-distance spring running event in the south Houston area by Bigfoot Running Company, offering a Kids Dash, 1-mile, 5K, 10K, and half marathon. The course location is TBD in the Houston 77047 area.

name: Race for the Dome
description: The 6th Annual Race for the Dome celebrates the Astrodome's 61st birthday with a 5K road course through NRG Park and a Kids 1K fun run. Participants are encouraged to wear vintage gear or Dome attire; the event benefits the Astrodome Conservancy.

name: Run for the Rose 5K
description: A 5K road race in Missouri City in Fort Bend County, part of a springtime rose-themed community running event.

name: AGPMA Run for the Goal 5K
description: The 13th Annual AGPMA Run for the Goal 5K takes place at Challenger Seven Memorial Park in Webster, benefiting Al Garza's Premier Martial Arts Black Belt Candidate class. A community-focused fundraiser 5K open to runners and walkers of all abilities.

name: Project Joy and Hope Fun Run
description: The 4th Annual Project Joy and Hope 5K Fun Run/Walk takes place at the San Jacinto Monument with stunning views of the San Jacinto Battleground. Proceeds support Project Joy and Hope, a nonprofit dedicated to helping seriously ill children and their families in the greater Houston area.

name: Sienna Run for the Rosé 5K
description: A chip-timed 5K through the Sienna master-planned community centered around Sawmill Lake in Missouri City, with a finish-line rosé and brunch celebration. Entry benefits Child Advocates of Fort Bend, a local nonprofit serving foster children.

name: Terry Hershey parkrun
description: A free, weekly timed 5K community run held every Saturday at 8:00 AM in Terry Hershey Park along the Buffalo Bayou trail in west Houston. Open to runners of all abilities with no pre-registration required; results are published online for all finishers.

name: Houston Empower Walk & 5K Run
description: A running race in Spring, TX.

name: Sheldon Shuffle 5K
description: A running race in Houston, TX.

name: Run for the Rose
description: A free-to-register 5K run and Family 1K at Helix Park in the Texas Medical Center, benefiting brain cancer research and patient support through the Dr. Marnie Rose Foundation. The event includes a Survivor Stroll at 7:45 AM and welcomes walkers alongside runners.

name: Saint Arnold Art Car IPA 5K presented by UHD
description: Houston's most festive 5K, coinciding with Art Car Parade weekend and starting and finishing at Saint Arnold Brewing Company. Participants receive a finisher medal, pint glass, and their choice of Saint Arnold beverages; the course winds through Houston's Fifth Ward neighborhood.

name: Vintage Park 13.1
description: A half marathon road race in the Vintage Park area of northwest Houston, running through the Champions/Willowbrook corridor.

name: B52 Brewing 5K Beer Run
description: A 5K beer run starting and finishing at B52 Brewing in Conroe, with two pints included post-race and food trucks on site.

name: Bearkat Bash
description: A community 5K road race held in the Klein area of Spring, Texas.

name: Gulf Coast Grind
description: A multi-distance race on packed sand along Surfside Beach on the Texas Gulf Coast, offering distances from 5K through 100K. Courses run along the water's edge with full aid stations.

name: Steps for Vets 5K Run/Walk/Roll
description: A peer-to-peer fundraising 5K run, walk, and roll benefiting U.S.VETS Houston, supporting the organization's mission to prevent and end veteran homelessness.

name: Sisters Network Inc. 16th Annual Stop the Silence 5K Walk/Run
description: The 16th Annual Stop the Silence 5K Walk/Run at Tom Bass Regional Park benefits the Karen E. Jackson Breast Cancer Assistance Program, supporting Black women affected by breast cancer. The Sisters Network Inc. event attracts approximately 3,000 participants and raises awareness for early detection and support resources.

name: Terry Hershey parkrun
description: A free, weekly timed 5K community run held every Saturday at 8:00 AM in Terry Hershey Park along the Buffalo Bayou trail in west Houston. Open to runners of all abilities with no pre-registration required; results are published online for all finishers.

name: XKLusive '06K Race/Walk
description: The Alexandria Foundation's '06K Race adds an extra 1K to a standard 5K to honor the 1906 founding year of Alpha Phi Alpha Fraternity, Inc., held at Brazos River Park in Sugar Land. Now in its fifth year, 2026 proceeds benefit local Autism nonprofits Hope for Three and Seensory and Me Ministries.

name: SJH Sabercat Stampede
description: A running race in Katy, TX.

name: Space City Gym 5k/10k
description: A running race in Humble, TX.

name: Space City Half Marathon, 10k, 5k, & Mile
description: The debut Space City Half Marathon makes its first appearance as the Season Opener of the Lone Star Race Series, offering a flat, fast course through Houston's westside inner-loop. The event is produced by the team behind the popular Houston Turkey Dash and includes half marathon, 10K, 5K, and mile distances.

name: Running through the Decades Half Marathon - Houston
description: The 1980s Edition of Bigfoot Running Company's themed half marathon series, set at Brazos River Park in Sugar Land with a pre-race costume contest and 80s music throughout. Distances include a Kids Dash, 1 Mile, 5K, Quarter Marathon (6.55 miles), and Half Marathon.

name: Inaugural Kalayaan 5K Fun Run
description: A running race in Houston, TX.

name: Bayou Bash Relay
description: A 4-person x 2.2 mile relay at T.C. Jester Park presented by the Bayou City Road Runners on paved and crushed granite trails. A HARRA Spring Series race followed by the HARRA end-of-year family picnic.

name: DoNot Stop Half Marathon (5k/10k) - Houston
description: A donut-themed half marathon, 10K, 5K, 1-mile, and Kids Dash held at John Paul Landing Park & Education Center in Cypress. Every participant receives a designer shirt, finisher medal, chip timing with live results, and free race-day photos, plus a donut waiting at the finish line.

name: Galveston Island Brewing Co 5K Beer Run
description: A chip-timed 5K beer run starting and finishing at Galveston Island Brewing Co., running along the Seawall on a flat out-and-back course with ocean views. Entry includes a souvenir pint glass and post-race beer tastings.

name: Smilin' Rylen Fun Run
description: Annual charity run at the University of Houston at Katy campus supporting the Smilin' Rylen Foundation, which raises awareness about organ donation and assists families in crisis. Offers 5K, 10K, and 1-mile distances; 10K and 5K start at 8:15 AM, 1-mile at approximately 9:00 AM.

name: 17th Annual Strides for Schools Fun Run
description: A community 5K fun run starting and finishing at the parking lot of Clyde Abshier Stadium near Deer Park High School South Campus, presented by Shell Deer Park. All proceeds benefit the Deer Park Education Foundation's teacher enhancement grant program.

name: Divas Galveston Half Marathon and 5K
description: A women's half marathon and 5K held along Galveston's iconic Seawall Boulevard, starting and finishing at Beach Central Park. Part of the national Divas Running Series, this flat coastal course runs along the Gulf of Mexico from start to finish, with glamorous finisher swag including a tutu, tiara, and medal.

name: Vintage Half Marathon, 5K & Kid's K presented by Houston Methodist
description: A half marathon, 5K, and Kids 1K starting and finishing at Klein High School in the Klein/Spring area of north Houston, part of the Run Houston Race Series presented by Houston Methodist. The race draws 1,000–5,000 participants across its distance options.

name: Biz Run
description: A running race in Sugarland, TX.

name: Move for MDS
description: A running race in Houston, TX.

name: Run Like a Diva
description: A running race in Galveston, TX.

name: Girls on the Run Greater Houston Celebratory 5K
description: The seasonal Girls on the Run Greater Houston Celebratory 5K marks the culmination of the spring program season, open to girl participants and their running buddies.

name: Klein ISD Education Foundation 5K and Kids Fun Run
description: A USATF-certified 5K race and Kids Fun Run benefiting the Klein ISD Education Foundation's teacher classroom grants, starting and finishing at Klein Memorial Stadium at Klein High School. The 5K starts at 7:30 AM; the Kids Fun Run (ages 5–12) is a 600-meter race around the stadium track at 8:45 AM.

name: The Pear Run
description: Celebrating its 20th anniversary, this annual 5K and 10K race is held at Independence Park in Pearland, with both courses USATF certified. The 10K starts at 7:30 AM and the 5K at 8:00 AM; also features a Kids Fun Run and the debut of the Pear Ruck for 2026.

name: Cinco De Mayo Run 5K/10K/13.1 HOUSTON
description: A Cinco de Mayo-themed race offering half marathon, 10K, 5K, and family 1-mile fun run distances along the Bayou Loop Trails in west Houston. Virtual run options are also available for those who prefer to participate remotely.

name: 2026 Preventive Medicine Initiative: Heart & Sole 5K
description: A running race in Galveston, TX.

name: Astros Race to the Pennant
description: A running race in Houston, TX.

name: Fuzzy's Taco Dash
description: A running race in Kingwood, TX.

name: RUN FOR HOPE
description: A running race in Spring, TX.

name: Run Like A Warrior 5k -Houston Area
description: A running race in Tomball, TX.

name: El Cinco Cinco
description: A Cinco de Mayo-themed 5K and 10K starting and finishing at Karbach Brewing Company in northwest Houston, with a Kids & Family 1K at 9:15 AM. Post-race festivities include live music, a Cinco de Mayo market, and refreshments; proceeds benefit Ainsley's Angels Southeast Texas, The Asher House Animal Sanctuary, and LifeGift Organ Donation.

name: Fuzzy's Taco Dash
description: A taco-themed 5K and 10K held at Fuzzy's Taco Shop in Sugar Land, with 10K relay team options available. Includes a Kids Dash for children ages 10 and under.

name: Sprint for Life
description: An annual 5K road race held at the Texas Medical Center in Houston supporting health and wellness initiatives.

name: Texas Crab Festival 5K
description: A timed 5K run/walk and Kids 1K held as part of the annual Texas Crab Festival on the Bolivar Peninsula. The festival runs May 7-9 at Swedes Park in Crystal Beach with live music, food, and vendors.

name: YCF 5K Fun Run
description: A 5K fun run on Galveston Island organized by the Young Champions Foundation.

name: Galveston Sand Crab 5K/10K/Kid's Beach 1K
description: A nighttime beach run at East Beach / Apffel Park in Galveston, with the 5K and 10K starting at 8:30 PM and the Kid's Beach Mile at 8:00 PM. The course is lit with LED lights; runners must carry a flashlight or headlamp. Part of the Run In Texas race series.

name: 29th Annual Sprint for Life 5K Run/Walk
description: The 29th Annual Sprint for Life 5K at Helix Park in the Texas Medical Center raises funds for the Blanton-Davis Ovarian Cancer Research Program at MD Anderson Cancer Center. Includes a Sprint for Sprouts/Tot Trot kids event at 9:30 AM.

name: Yaga's Children's Fund WILD 5K Fun Run
description: A 5K fun run at Moody Gardens in Galveston kicking off Yaga's Wild Game & BBQ Cook-Off weekend. All proceeds benefit Yaga's Children's Fund, supporting charities that serve the children of Galveston County. Post-race BBQ, live music, and vendors follow at 11 AM.

name: The Race to Grand Oaks
description: A running race in Spring, TX.

name: Hometown Half Marathon & 5K/10K - Houston
description: A multi-distance race at Brazos River Park in Sugar Land offering a Kid's Dash, 1 Mile, 5K, 10K, and Half Marathon. The flat course follows the Brazos River and includes chip timing, pacers for the half marathon, and a post-race celebration.

name: Legacy of Courage Half Marathon - Houston
description: A trail half marathon through the 320-acre Burroughs Park in Tomball on well-maintained hiking trails beneath a canopy of pine trees. Also offers 1 Mile, 5K, and Kids Dash options. Proceeds benefit The Outdoor Disabled Veterans Program through Knowledge Point Network.

name: 5K Mamas Fun Run
description: A running race in Missouri City, TX.

name: Houston Humane Society 43rd Annual K-9 Fun Run + Walk
description: The 43rd Annual Houston Humane Society K-9 Fun Run in the Sawyer Yards area welcomes runners and their dogs in a 5K benefiting animal welfare programs.

name: Santa Fe Ten Memorial Run In Remembrance
description: A 5K run/walk and Kids 1K in Hitchcock, TX honoring the ten victims lost in the May 18, 2018 Santa Fe High School shooting. Proceeds benefit the Santa Fe Ten Memorial. Includes music, a silent auction, food trucks, and age-group awards.

name: Terlingua Track Club LP Run
description: Billed as the oldest race in Texas, the Terlingua Track Club LP Run at Rice University has all participants run as far as possible in exactly 33 minutes and 20 seconds. A HARRA Spring Series event.

name: Operation: Ruck the Bayou
description: The 2nd annual Operation: Ruck the Bayou at Saint Arnold Brewery offers 5K and 10K run and ruck options plus a Kids/Family K. Proceeds support The Joshua Chamberlain Society's Houston Chapter, which provides long-term support to veterans with permanent combat injuries and Gold Star families.

name: The Wiener Run 5K & 10K - Houston
description: A dachshund-themed 5K and 10K at Oyster Creek Park in Sugar Land with a Kids Dash for ages 10 and under and a 1 Mile option. Every finisher gets a complimentary hot dog and beverage at the post-race BBQ bash with live music. Supports a local wiener dog rescue group.

name: Mindy Moses 5K Memorial Fun Run
description: A running race in Bellville, TX.

name: Run Houston! Clear Lake 5K & 10K presented by Houston Methodist
description: The third race in the Run Houston! Spring Series, at Clear Lake near NASA. Includes timed and untimed 5K and 10K plus a Kid's 1K for ages 1-12. The series partners with community nonprofits to provide sneakers and socks to underserved children.

name: Fit Foodie Family 5K & Kids' 1K
description: A spring fitness and food-themed 5K and Kids' 1K at Sugar Land Town Square on Memorial Day weekend. Post-race party features live entertainment, massages, family activities, and vendor tastings.

name: Run for Mental Health 5K/10K/13.1 Houston
description: A mental health awareness run offering 5K, 10K, half marathon, and 1-mile family fun run distances in Houston. Includes optional chip timing, a finisher's medal, and race T-shirt. Venue listed as Eleanor Tinsley Park or Bayou Loop Trails area.

name: Daily News Press Run - Texas City
description: A running race in Texas City, TX.

name: Run To Rebuild Texas - 5k Fun Run
description: A running race in New Caney, TX.

name: Memorial Day Half Marathon, 10K & 5K
description: The 5th race in Houston Running Co.'s Holiday Series, a Memorial Day Half Marathon (7:00 a.m. start), 10K, and 5K (both 7:30 a.m.) in the Washington Avenue Corridor area honoring the men and women who gave their lives serving in the U.S. Military.

name: Memorial Day Run
description: Houston Running Co.'s annual Memorial Day event at St. Thomas High School honoring fallen service members. Features a scenic Memorial Drive route with half marathon (7:00 AM), 5K/10K (7:30 AM), and Kids/Family 1K (9:15 AM). Part of the Houston Holiday Series.

name: Memorial Day Hero Salute
description: The 2nd Annual Memorial Day Hero Salute at Town Center Park in Kingwood offers a half marathon, 10K, 5K, and Kids Mini-Salute, plus optional MURPH and LOREDO military fitness challenges. Organized by Rad Racing Company to honor fallen service members. Post-race includes a kids and winners tug-of-war.

name: Pasadena Police Department Superhero 5K/Kids 1K
description: A superhero-themed 5K and Kids 1K at the Pasadena Police Academy. Participants are encouraged to wear superhero costumes. The Kids 1K for ages 12 and under starts at 8:30 AM. Benefits the Pasadena Police Department community programs.

name: Tejas Beer 5K
description: An early-morning 5K starting and finishing at Tejas Brewery in the Sawyer Yards neighborhood with views of the downtown Houston skyline. Part of the Run In Texas 2026 Texas 5K 6-Pack Beer Challenge. Entry includes a finisher glass, four tastings, and a performance fabric race tee.

name: Lemonade Dash Houston
description: The 6th Annual Lemonade Dash kicks off Houston Running Co.'s Summer Dash Series at Karbach Brewing Co. Includes 5K, 10K, and Kids/Family Fun Run. Post-race party with lemonade-themed refreshments and free parking at the brewery.

name: Miles for Miracles: 5K for NICU Families
description: A 5K fun run supporting the Harmonie Grace Foundation and families of micro preemies. Family-friendly event open to all ages and fitness levels, raising awareness and funds for NICU families.

name: Big Rivers Splash & Dash
description: A 5K, 10K, and Kid's Dash at Big Rivers Waterpark in New Caney. The scenic course winds through the waterpark and around the lake. Proceeds support research and awareness for TANGO2, a rare genetic disorder.

name: 2026 Outrun Hunger
address: The Lake House at Grand Central Park
description: A running race in Conroe, TX.

name: Houston Stars & Stripes Half Marathon, 10K & 5K
description: A patriotic-themed half marathon, 10K, and 5K at Atascocita High School in Humble benefiting the PTSD Foundation of America, with runners receiving an American flag to carry across the finish line. Race 6 of 9 in the Run Houston Race Series.

name: No Label Brewing 1st Street 5K Beer Run
description: A 5K beer run starting and finishing at No Label Brewing in downtown Katy. Part of the Run In Texas 2026 Texas 5K 6-Pack Beer Challenge. Entry includes a souvenir pint glass, four 8-oz tastings, a race tee, and food trucks on site.

name: Houston Stars and Stripes Half Marathon, 10K, 5K & Kid's K presented by Houston Methodist
description: A patriotic race in the Run Houston series where finishers receive an American flag to wave across the finish line, plus finisher medals and bomb pops. Half marathon starts at 7:05 AM, 5K/10K at 7:15 AM. Timed and untimed 5K options available.

name: Independence Day 8K & 5K
description: The 10th Annual Independence Day 8K & 5K at Meyerland Plaza is the 6th race in the Houston Holiday Series. The 8K and 5K start at 7:00 a.m. on a fast, mostly flat patriotic-themed course, followed by a Kids and Family 1K at 9:00 a.m.

name: Independence Day 8K
description: Houston Running Co.'s 10th annual Independence Day 8K and 5K at Meyerland Plaza. The 8K (not a standard 5K/10K distance) and 5K start at 7:00 AM with an award ceremony at 8:45 AM and Kids/Family 1K at 9:00 AM. Part of the Houston Holiday Series.

name: Firecracker 5K
description: A one-time-only, USATF-sanctioned evening 5K celebrating America's 250th anniversary, held along The Woodlands Waterway on July 2. Produced by The Woodlands Township Parks and Recreation Department, the chip-timed course starts at 7 p.m. with no race-day registration available.

name: Houston Fourth Fest Road Race
description: The 2nd annual Houston Fourth Fest features a flat, mostly shaded July 4th road race at Vintage Park in NW Houston with distances of 5K, 9K, 17.76K (commemorating 1776), and a family 1.776K fun run. The event also hosts Houston's Beer Mile Championship and Hot Dog Eating Contest Championship post-race.

name: Run Wild 5K & Kids Run
description: A beloved annual July 4th 5K in Houston's Uptown Park neighborhood benefiting Special Olympics Texas and Girls on the Run Greater Houston. The chip-timed course runs through the Uptown area near I-610 and Post Oak, with a separate kids' 0.6-mile fun run; typically sells out well in advance.

name: Texas Bud Heat Wave
description: A July 4th tradition east of Houston offering a 5K and 5-mile run on a flat, out-and-back road course starting and finishing at Wismer Distributing in Mont Belvieu. Post-race festivities connect with the city's Independence Day celebration featuring food, live music, and a patriotic costume contest.

name: July 4th Run Houston 5K/10K/Half Marathon
description: A small-group July 4th road race in Houston offering 5K, 10K, and Half Marathon distances starting at 7:30 a.m. plus a Family 1 Mile Fun Run at 9:00 a.m. 10% of net proceeds plus 100% of fundraising goes to Hope for the Warriors.

name: Kingwood Texas 4th of July Freedom Run
description: The 3rd annual Kingwood July 4th Freedom Run takes participants through the scenic Kingwood Greenbelt Trails with 8-mile, 4-mile, and 2-mile options plus a Kids Dash, starting from Town Center Park. Organized by Rad Racing Company with a patriotic attire contest and charity support for Team RWB and the Torch of Freedom Foundation.

name: Houston Fourth Fest
description: The 2nd annual Houston Fourth Fest at Vintage Park featuring a flat, mostly-shaded road race (5K, 9K, and 17.76K loops plus a Family 1.776K), Houston's Beer Mile Championship, a hot dog eating contest, and a post-race celebration with free beer, pizza, and live music from 104.1 KRBE.

name: Treat Trot Houston
description: A summer trot offering 5K, 10K, and 15K distances in Houston. Includes a designer shirt, custom medal, chip timing with live results, free photos, and snacks at the finish line. Youth pricing available for ages 12 and under.

name: Eagle 5K, 10K & 13.1M at Katy, TX (28)
description: Part of the US Road Running Eagle Series, this summer race at Mason Creek Hike & Bike Trail in Katy offers 5K, 10K, and half marathon distances on a flat out-and-back course with a 3.5-hour time limit. Chip-timed with finisher medals; no on-site registration available.

name: Eagle 5K, 10K & 13.1M at Cypress, TX (28)
description: Part of the US Road Running Eagle Series, this weekly race at John Paul Landing Park in Cypress features 5K, 10K, and half marathon on a flat out-and-back course with a 3.5-hour time limit. No on-site registration; chip-timed with finisher medals.

name: Popsicle Dash Houston
description: The 7th Annual Popsicle Dash at Karbach Brewing Co., the second race in Houston Running Co.'s Summer Dash Series. Includes 5K, 10K, and Kids/Family Fun Run with free parking at the brewery.

name: Coastal Plains Relay
description: A running race in Richmond, TX.

name: Eagle 5K, 10K & 13.1M at Deer Park, TX (29)
description: Part of the US Road Running Eagle Series, this weekly race at Dow Park in Deer Park offers 5K, 10K, and half marathon on a flat out-and-back course with a 3.5-hour time limit. Chip-timed with finisher medals; no on-site registration.

name: Lunar Landing 5K & Kids 1K
description: A 5K and Kids 1K at the Clear Lake Recreation and Community Center near NASA, celebrating the Apollo 11 moon landing. Kids 1K starts at 7:00 AM with the 5K immediately after. Part of the Texas 5K Series. 90-minute time limit.

name: Red, White and Blue Half Marathon - Houston
description: Hosted by Bigfoot Running Company at Kingdom Builders Center in southwest Houston, this summer race offers a half marathon, 8-mile, 4-mile, 2-mile, and kids' dash with chip timing and custom medals. Top finishers in the longer distances earn a coveted Bigfoot trophy.

name: Eagle 5K, 10K & 13.1M at Cypress, TX (30)
description: Part of the US Road Running Eagle Series, this weekly race at John Paul Landing Park in Cypress offers 5K, 10K, and half marathon on a flat out-and-back course. No on-site registration; chip-timed with finisher medals and a 3.5-hour course limit.

name: Triple Dog Dare
description: A summer road race at Sugar Land Memorial Park offering 5K, 10K, and 15K distances with chip timing, custom finisher medals, and age group awards four deep. Runners who register by July 18 are guaranteed their shirt size.

name: Christmas in July 5K & 10K Houston
description: A Christmas-themed summer 5K and 10K in Houston. Part of the seasonal themed race series popular in the Houston area.

name: Shark Week Showdown 5K, 10K - Galveston, TX
description: A running race in Galveston, TX.

name: Terrain Race | Houston
description: A 5K obstacle race through the natural terrain of Three Palms Action Sports Park north of Houston, featuring 20+ obstacles including mud, ropes, monkey bars, and walls. Runners select a start wave and compete on the same course used for all ability levels.

name: Heatwave Half Marathon - Houston
description: A mid-summer multi-distance race offering a Kids Dash, 1 Mile, 5K, 10K, half marathon, and half marathon relay. Chip timing, custom medals, and a post-race celebration.

name: Watermelon Dash Houston
description: The 7th Annual Watermelon Dash at Karbach Brewing Co., the third and final race in Houston Running Co.'s Summer Dash Series. 5K and 10K start at 7:00 AM, award ceremony at 8:45 AM, Kids/Family 1K at 9:00 AM. Early packet pickup at Sun & Ski Sports Westheimer.

name: Houston Party in the Park
description: A free annual community celebration organized by HARRA (Houston Area Road Runners Association) at Memorial Park, featuring a Houston Striders 5K at 8 a.m. and a Chevron Houston Marathon Kids Fun Run (1-mile and 3-mile). The event includes live music, free refreshments, a DJ, and running club showcases.

name: Ninja 5K, 10K & 13.1M at Katy, TX (32)
description: Part of the US Road Running series, this weekly race at Mason Creek Hike & Bike Trail in Katy offers 5K, 10K, and half marathon on a flat out-and-back course. No on-site registration; chip-timed with finisher medals and a 3.5-hour course limit.

name: Southern Star Brewing Co. 5K Beer Run
description: Event #6 in the Run In Texas 5K Six Pack Beer Challenge, this annual 5K beer run starts and finishes at Southern Star Brewing Company in Conroe. Benefiting Toys for Tots, the flat course winds through the Sunset Ridge neighborhood; entry includes a souvenir pint glass and post-race beer tastings.

name: Summer Meltdown Half Marathon - Houston
description: A summer half marathon at Brazos River Park in Sugar Land, also offering a quarter marathon (6.55 miles), 5K, 1 Mile, and Kids Dash. All distances start at 7:00 AM with chip timing and custom medals.

name: Habanero Hundred
description: Known as Texas' hottest trail race, the Habanero Hundred is a TROT-produced multi-day event held at 7IL Ranch near Bellville offering 5K, 10K, half marathon, marathon, 50K, 100K, 100-mile, and a new 135-mile distance. The ultra distances start at noon on Friday; shorter distances start Saturday on a looped 10K trail course with roughly 280 feet of elevation gain per loop.

name: Shark Week Showdown 5K & 10K - Houston
description: An ocean-themed summer race hosted by Bigfoot Running Company at Oyster Creek Park in Sugar Land, featuring a kids' dash, 1-mile, 5K, and 10K with chip timing and custom finisher medals. Top finishers in the 5K and 10K receive a Bigfoot Trophy, and all participants can expect music and themed festivities.

name: 2nd Annual Run & Brews Fun Run at Katy Beer Garden
description: A fun summer run-and-beer event at Katy Beer Garden offering 1-mile, 5K, and 10K distances with post-race brews in Katy. Venue address to be confirmed on the race website closer to the event.

name: Summer Sizzler - Greater Houston
description: A late-summer Bodies Race Company event at Brazos River Park in Sugar Land offering 1-mile, 5K, 10K, and 15K distances with chip timing, custom finisher medals, designer shirts, Kona Ice, and a kids' dash. Registration by the prior Sunday is required to guarantee a shirt size.

name: Medal Madness 5K, 10K & 13.1M at Deer Park, TX (35)
description: Part of the US Road Running Medal Madness series, this race at Dow Park in Deer Park offers 5K, 10K, and half marathon on a flat out-and-back course. No on-site registration; chip-timed with a 3.5-hour limit and themed finisher medals.

name: Medal Madness 5K, 10K & 13.1M at Katy, TX (35)
description: Part of the US Road Running Medal Madness series, this race at Mason Creek Hike & Bike Trail in Katy offers 5K, 10K, and half marathon on a flat out-and-back course. No on-site registration; chip-timed with themed finisher medals and a 3.5-hour course limit.

name: APWA - PWX 2026 - 5K
description: A running race in Houston, TX.

name: BENeezy Purple Monkey Fun Run
description: Annual fun run in Alvin honoring the memory of Ben Garcia, raising scholarship funds for Alvin High School student athletes. Features a 1K kids run, 5K, and 10K. Produced by Running Alliance Sport.

name: Chance for Hope Going Gold Fun Run
description: A timed 5K and 10K in Pearland supporting the Chance for Hope Foundation, which provides services for children with cancer and their families and funds pediatric cancer research. Features an event village with music, raffle prizes, and children's activities.

name: Texas Gatorfest Twilight 5K
description: A twilight 5K run/walk at Fort Anahuac Park as the sun sets, held during the annual Texas Gatorfest weekend. Check-in at 5:00 PM, race at 6:00 PM. Registration $30, closes August 14.

name: Tunnel to Towers 5K Run & Walk - Cypress, TX / Northwest Houston
description: The 4th annual Tunnel to Towers 5K at John Paul Landing Park in Cypress, honoring the memory of FDNY firefighter Stephen Siller and saluting local first responders and military. Stroller and wheelchair friendly.

name: 9/11 Heroes Run - Houston, Texas
description: A 5K run and ruck event paying tribute to the heroes of September 11th and honoring those who have served since. Benefits Assist the Officer, IAFF Local 341, and Operation Lone Star.

name: Run Houston! at University of Houston 5K & 10K presented by Houston Methodist
description: Part of the Run Houston Race Series, this 5K, 10K, and Kids K is set on the University of Houston campus. The series partners with community nonprofits to provide sneakers and socks to underserved children.

name: Medal Madness 5K, 10K & 13.1M at Katy, TX (37)
description: Part of the US Road Running Medal Madness series, this race at Mason Creek Hike & Bike Trail in Katy offers 5K, 10K, and half marathon on a flat out-and-back course. No on-site registration; chip-timed with themed finisher medals and a 3.5-hour course limit.

name: Medal Madness 5K, 10K & 13.1M at Cypress, TX (37)
description: Part of the US Road Running Medal Madness series at John Paul Landing Park in Cypress, offering 5K, 10K, and half marathon on a flat out-and-back course along a park trail. No on-site registration; chip-timed with themed finisher medals.

name: Night Moves Trail Races
description: A running race in San Felipe, TX.

name: Run for a New Day
description: A running race in Galveston, TX.

name: Toughest 10K Kemah
description: The kickoff race of the Texas Bridge Series, taking runners over the Kemah-Seabrook Bridge with a 3.5% grade and views of Galveston Bay and Clear Lake. Finishes at the Kemah Boardwalk. Strict 2-hour time limit. No strollers or dogs on course.

name: Heroes and Villains Half Marathon - Houston
description: A superhero-and-villain themed race hosted by Bigfoot Running Company in the Sugar Land area, offering kids' dash, 1-mile, 5K, quarter marathon, and half marathon with chip timing, costume contest, and Bigfoot Trophy for top finishers. Venue to be confirmed closer to race day.

name: SUGAR LAND SPACE COWBOYS 5K
description: A running race in Sugar Land, TX.

name: 16th Annual Woodforest Charity Run
description: A 5K, 10K, and kids' dash at Heritage Place Park in downtown Conroe benefiting the Woodforest Charitable Foundation. All net proceeds support the foundation's community programs.

name: Medal Madness 5K, 10K & 13.1M at Cypress, TX (39)
description: Part of the US Road Running Medal Madness series at John Paul Landing Park in Cypress, offering 5K, 10K, and half marathon on a flat out-and-back course. No on-site registration; chip-timed with themed finisher medals and a 3.5-hour course limit.

name: Golden Harvest Half Marathon & 5K/10K - Houston
description: A fall-season road race in Houston offering kids' dash, 1-mile, 5K, 10K, and half marathon with chip timing, custom finisher medals, and free photos. Produced by Bodies Race Company; venue to be confirmed closer to race day.

name: Turkey Trails - Houston
description: A multi-distance race at Oyster Creek Park in Sugar Land offering a Kids Dash, 1 Mile, 5K, 10K, and Half Marathon. Custom soft t-shirt and finisher medal for all participants.

name: Galveston Island Shrimp Festival 5K
description: A 5K fun run on Galveston Island held during the annual Shrimp Festival. Warm-up at 8:45 AM, 5K start at 9:00 AM. A portion of proceeds benefits The Justin Hughes Fund at UTMB for medical student depression awareness.

name: Houston - HFTC 5k
description: A running race in Houston, TX.

name: Zeal RumRunners Race Series
description: A high-energy community timed race series at Meridiana Pkwy in Rosharon, featuring 10K (8:00 AM), 5K (8:15 AM), and a Kids 1K, presented by Zeal Premium Rum. All 21+ finishers receive a complimentary rum cocktail at the post-race celebration.

name: The Rad Race & Rad Fest (Fall Edition)
description: A multi-sport fall festival celebrating BMX culture featuring 5K and 10K running races, a kids' dash, a bike race experience, and the RAD Zone festival area with music, vendors, food, and a costume contest. Held along the Kingwood Country Club Trails and organized in partnership with H-Town BMX.

name: Ainsley's Angels 9th Annual Twilight 5K
description: A running race in Tomball, TX.

name: Ainsley's Angels Texas Twilight 5K
description: A twilight 5K in Tomball benefiting Ainsley's Angels, an organization providing racing wheelchairs and inclusivity for athletes with disabilities.

name: Haunted 5K, 10K & 13.1M at Katy, TX (41)
description: Part of the US Road Running Haunted series at Mason Creek Hike & Bike Trail in Katy, offering 5K, 10K, and half marathon on a flat out-and-back course with themed finisher medals. No on-site registration; chip-timed with a 3.5-hour course limit.

name: Day of the Dead Half Marathon - Galveston
description: A running race in Galveston, TX.

name: Sugar Land Half Marathon, 10K & Mile
description: The 5th annual Houston Methodist Sugar Land Half Marathon, celebrating year five of the Lone Star Race Series and kicking off the 2026-2027 season. The mostly flat single-loop course through Sugar Land's residential streets starts and finishes near Smart Financial Centre and features a kids' half marathon, full wheelchair and handcycle divisions, and a post-race party with live music and food.

name: Space City 10 Miler & 5K
description: A HARRA Fall Series event at the University of Houston – Clear Lake campus, featuring a certified 10-mile single loop through the scenic Clear Lake area past Armand Bayou and NASA, plus a 5K and 2-person relay option. Cash prizes up to $500 for overall winners, long-sleeve shirts for all entrants, and finisher medals.

name: 40th Annual Houston Heights Fun Run
description: The 40th annual Houston Heights Fun Run starting and finishing at Marmion Park on Heights Boulevard. Features a 10K, 5K, 5K Walk, and Kids Fun Run (~1K). A beloved Heights neighborhood tradition since 1984.

name: Space City 10 Miler and 5K
description: A HARRA Fall Series race at UH-Clear Lake campus with a 10-mile loop through the Clear Lake area and a 5K option. Cash awards for top finishers. Also offers a 2-person relay.

name: Child Advocates 5K and 1K Superhero Run
description: A superhero-themed 5K and Kids 1K at CityCentre in west Houston benefiting Child Advocates, Houston's court-appointed special advocates for children.

name: Breast Reconstruction Awareness Close the Loop 5K 2026
description: A running race in Houston, TX.

name: Shipley Donut Dash
description: Houston's sweetest fun run, held at Sam Houston Race Park and benefiting Kids' Meals, offering a 10K at 7:00 AM, 5K at 8:00 AM, and kids' dash. All participants receive a donut-shaped finisher medal, race bib, Shipley Do-Nuts, and coffee; two on-course donut stations add to the festive atmosphere.

name: Day of the Dead Half Marathon - Houston
description: A Día de los Muertos–themed half marathon hosted by Bigfoot Running Company at Brazos River Park in Sugar Land, offering kids' dash, 1-mile, 5K, quarter marathon, and half marathon. Features a pre-race costume contest, DJ, Bigfoot trophies for top finishers, and a festive post-race celebration at the Race Rally Tent.

name: Haunted 5K, 10K & 13.1M at Deer Park, TX (42)
description: Part of the US Road Running Haunted series at Dow Park in Deer Park, offering 5K, 10K, and half marathon on a flat out-and-back course with themed finisher medals. No on-site registration; chip-timed with a 3.5-hour course limit.

name: 2026 Miles 4 Matthew 5K/10K
description: An annual fundraiser honoring Matthew, a young boy who lost his life at age 9 to leukemia, held at No Label Brewing Co. in Katy. Proceeds provide financial support to families battling pediatric cancer; participants choose from 5K, 10K, or a free kids 1K fun run.

name: Friends of Mae S. Bruce Library Fun Run
description: A running race in Santa Fe, TX.

name: Galveston Ultra Trail
description: A running race in Galveston, TX.

name: Angels' Race For Space 5K
description: A 5K at Space Center Houston near the Johnson Space Center campus.

name: Toughest 10k Galveston
description: A running race in Galveston, TX.

name: CHORD ENERGY HOUSTON CORPORATE 5K
address: Start Line Location: Eastern Glades (East Memorial Park Loop) - Flat and Fast, One Lap Around Seymour Lieberman Trail Loop
description: A running race in Houston, TX.

name: Haunted 5K, 10K & 13.1M at Cypress, TX (43)
description: Part of the US Road Running Haunted series at John Paul Landing Park in Cypress, offering 5K, 10K, and half marathon on a flat out-and-back course with themed finisher medals. No on-site registration; chip-timed with a 3.5-hour course limit.

name: Spooky Sprint - Houston
description: An annual Halloween-themed race hosted by Bodies Race Company at Brazos River Park in Sugar Land, offering kids' dash, 1-mile, 5K, 10K, and half marathon with chip timing and custom finisher medals. Features a costume contest, free donuts and treats at the finish line, and a Halloween after-party atmosphere.

name: UTHealth Houston Half Marathon & 10K
description: Hosted by the Houston Striders since 2006, this USATF-certified race starts at Sam Houston Park and follows a mostly flat downtown route along Allen Parkway and through the city. Title-sponsored by UTHealth Houston, the event raises over $1 million for area nonprofits and includes free Saint Arnold beer, IHOP pancakes, photo downloads, and wheelchair/handcycle divisions.

name: West U Halloween Dash and Kids' Fun Run
description: A Halloween-themed 5K and Kids Fun Run in West University Place, a tradition in one of Houston's most walkable inner-loop neighborhoods.

name: Medal Madness 5K, 10K & 13.1M at Deer Park, TX (44)
description: Part of the US Road Running Medal Madness series at Dow Park in Deer Park, offering 5K, 10K, and half marathon on a flat out-and-back course. No on-site registration; chip-timed with themed finisher medals and a 3.5-hour course limit.

name: Monster Mash
description: Race 7 of 9 in Houston Running Co.'s Houston Holiday Series, held Halloween morning at Karbach Brewing Co. and featuring a 5K, 10K, and kids & family 1K in full costume. One of Houston's most beloved Halloween events with costume judging, post-race live music, beer at Karbach Brewing, and the complete Monster Mash after-party.

name: Medal Madness 5K, 10K & 13.1M at Katy, TX (44)
description: Part of the US Road Running Medal Madness series at Mason Creek Hike & Bike Trail in Katy, offering 5K, 10K, and half marathon on a flat out-and-back course. No on-site registration; chip-timed with themed Halloween-season finisher medals and a 3.5-hour course limit.

name: Daily News Press Run - Galveston
description: A running race in Galveston, TX.

name: 2026 Xracers Day Of The Dead 10K (Ride and Run)
description: A Día de los Muertos–themed hybrid event at the beautiful Baytown Nature Center, combining a 5K trail run and a 10K bike-and-run distance. Participants enjoy the park's wildlife, trails, and waterfront scenery; all finishers receive a shirt and medal at the finish line.

name: Race For Permanent Sanctuary
description: A 5K in Sugar Land supporting permanent animal sanctuary efforts.

name: Harvest Marathon Relay
description: A marathon-distance relay race at Rice University, a popular Houston marathon training benchmark typically run in 4-person teams on the Rice outer loop.

name: 'WHATARUN 5K/10K' (Houston, TX)
description: Celebrating 75 years of Whataburger, this 2nd annual race brings burger lovers together for a flat and fast USATF-certified 5K and 10K on Park Row Drive at the Addicks Park & Ride in the Energy Corridor. Limited to 2,000 participants; entry includes officially licensed Whataburger race swag, post-race food truck sampling, and a visit from Whataguy.

name: Explore The Falls Race
description: A Veterans Day tradition in The Falls at Imperial Oaks community in Spring, blending 1 mile of road with gravel, grass, and trail sections around Lake Holcomb. The Kids 1K starts at 7:30 AM on a closed road; 5K and 10K follow with chip timing, age group awards, race photos, and a swag bag for all participants.

name: Back Pew Brewing Co. 5K Beer Run
description: Part of the Run In Texas 5K Six Pack Beer Challenge, this flat and fast out-and-back 5K starts and finishes at Back Pew Brewing Co. in Porter (NE Houston area), with one water stop benefiting Family Promise of Lake Houston. Entry includes a souvenir pint glass, four 8-oz beer tastings, food trucks, and custom awards for top finishers.

name: Big Tex Backyard Ultra
description: A running race in San Felipe, TX.

name: Fall Family Fun Run
description: A running race in Katy, TX.

name: Warriors of Hope - Irene's 5K
description: A running race in Spring, TX.

name: Turkey Shuffle - Houston
description: A Thanksgiving-themed run hosted by Bigfoot Running Company at Oyster Creek Park in Sugar Land, featuring a kids' dash, 1-mile, 5K, and 10K with chip timing, custom finisher medals, and DJ Sasquatch. Participants are encouraged to wear their best Turkey Day costumes for the pre-race costume contest.

name: Gobbler 5K, 10K & 13.1M at Katy, TX (46)
description: Part of the US Road Running Gobbler series at Mason Creek Hike & Bike Trail in Katy, offering 5K, 10K, and half marathon on a flat out-and-back course. No on-site registration; chip-timed with Thanksgiving-themed finisher medals and a 3.5-hour course limit.

name: Gobbler 5K, 10K & 13.1M at Cypress, TX (46)
description: Part of the US Road Running Gobbler series at John Paul Landing Park in Cypress, offering 5K, 10K, and half marathon on a flat out-and-back course. No on-site registration; chip-timed with themed finisher medals and a 3.5-hour course limit.

name: La Porte By the Bay Half Marathon
description: The third and final race of the Texas Bridge Series, this half marathon starts at Sylvan Beach Park in La Porte and crosses the iconic Fred Hartman Bridge — whose 440-foot yellow cable towers have been called an open-air cathedral — twice before finishing at Five Points Town Plaza. Organized by 3 Bros Running Company with a strict 4-hour time limit; all finishers receive a medal and post-race celebration with food and cold beer.

name: Great Houston Pie Run
description: Race 8 of 9 in Houston Running Co.'s Houston Holiday Series, held the Saturday before Thanksgiving at King's BierHaus in the Lazybrook/Timbergrove neighborhood. Runners choose from a half marathon, 10K, 5K, or kids/family 1K, then celebrate with cold beer, mini pie, and the satisfaction of earning dessert before Turkey Day.

name: Houston 25K
description: A running race in Houston, TX.

name: HMSA Classical 25K, 10 Mile & 5K
description: The 40th annual Houston 25K (15.5 miles), 10 Mile, and 5K at Sam Houston Park in downtown Houston. The third race in the Houston Marathon Warm-up Series, a key training benchmark for January marathon runners. Hosted by Houston Masters Sports Association.

name: Houston Turkey Dash
description: A Thanksgiving morning tradition organized by Houston Running Co. at Delmar Stadium in the Spring Branch area, offering half marathon, 10K, 5K, 1-mile, kids K, and a handcycle division. Proceeds support The HRC Foundation and partners including Ainsley's Angels, Girls on the Run, and LifeGift Organ Donation.

name: The Turkey Trot Presented by Saint Arnold Brewing Co.
description: Downtown Houston's Thanksgiving morning tradition at Saint Arnold Brewing Company, featuring a chip-timed 5K and 10K course with views of the Downtown skyline plus an inclusive 1-mile walk. Multiple wave starts run from 7:00–9:30 AM; proceeds benefit CoolxDad, a Houston nonprofit uplifting fathers and families.

name: Houston Methodist Turkey Trot
description: A Thanksgiving morning tradition of over 30 years starting near Dillard's at the Galleria and running a USATF-certified course through the Uptown/Galleria district of Houston. The 10K starts at 7:30 AM, 5K at 8:00 AM, and kids run at 9:00 AM; thousands of runners, walkers, and families participate annually.

name: Katy YMCAs 24th Annual Turkey Dash
description: A beloved 24-year Thanksgiving morning tradition organized by the Katy Area YMCAs near Cinco Ranch, featuring a 10K, 5K, 1-mile family walk/run, and 1-mile kids race (ages 3–12). All finishers receive medals, and proceeds fund YMCA scholarships for Katy community members.

name: Turkey Trails - Houston (Sugar Land)
description: A Thanksgiving-weekend celebration hosted by Bodies Race Company at Brazos River Park in Sugar Land, offering kids' dash, 1-mile, 5K, 10K, and half marathon with chip timing and custom finisher medals. The Saturday-after-Thanksgiving timing is perfect for runners who prefer celebrating a bit later in the holiday weekend.

name: Gobbler 5K, 10K & 13.1M at Cypress, TX (48)
description: Part of the US Road Running Gobbler series at John Paul Landing Park in Cypress, offering 5K, 10K, and half marathon on a flat out-and-back course the Saturday after Thanksgiving. No on-site registration; chip-timed with themed finisher medals and a 3.5-hour course limit.

name: Texas City Half Marathon, 10K, 5K & Family Mile
description: The December finale of the Lone Star Race Series, run on the traffic-free Texas City Dike with panoramic Galveston Bay views. The half marathon course traces the full 5-mile dike as an out-and-back, then finishes with a 3.1-mile loop through Texas City's shoreline park — flat and fast throughout.

name: Run Houston! Sugar Land Santa 5K & 10K presented by Houston Methodist
description: The holiday finale of the Run Houston! Race Series by Houston Methodist, starting and finishing at Constellation Field (Sugar Land Space Cowboys stadium). The festive 5K, 10K, and Kids 1K loop paved roads in the stadium district; proceeds benefit Fort Bend Habitat for Humanity.

name: Brazos Bend 100
description: Texas's largest trail race, held the first Saturday of December at Brazos Bend State Park in Needville. Events span 100 miles (a Western States 100 lottery qualifier for sub-32-hour finishers), 100K, marathon, and half marathon on a flat, wildlife-rich multi-loop course through bayou flats shared with American alligators. A free kids race runs at noon Saturday; the finish line closes Sunday at 2 PM.

name: A Charlie Brown Christmas 5K/10K
description: The 5th Annual officially licensed Peanuts event at Addicks Park and Ride in Houston's Energy Corridor. The flat, USATF-certified out-and-back course runs on Park Row Drive. Schedule: Snoopy Kids Dash 8:00 AM, 10K 8:15 AM, 5K 8:20 AM. Capped at 2,000 participants and sold out all four prior years. All finishers receive a limited-edition collector's snow globe medal, performance tee, and numbered Peanuts bib.

name: Last Man Standing
description: A running race in Willis, TX.

name: Run for the House - Ronald McDonald House Charities Greater Houston 5K and Kids 1 Miler
description: A running race in Houston, TX.

name: Gingerbread Lane 5K & 10K Houston
description: A festive holiday trail race at John Paul Landing Park along the Cypress greenbelt, featuring a 5K, 10K, untimed 1 Mile, and Kids Dash (50-meter sprint). DJ Sasquatch provides holiday music on course; post-race includes gingerbread cookies and milk. Costume contest with prizes. Part of the multi-city Bigfoot Running Company Gingerbread Lane series.

name: Trafigura Run for the House
description: The 16th annual USATF-certified 5K and Kids 1-Miler in downtown Houston benefiting Ronald McDonald House Charities Greater Houston. The Trafigura Foundation matches proceeds dollar for dollar up to $300,000.

name: Flapjack Fun Run 5K
description: A 5K fun run in Friendswood, a south-Houston suburb between Pearland and League City.

name: Tavola Reindeer Run
description: A holiday-themed reindeer run in the Tavola community in New Caney, northeast of Houston.

name: Jingle Bell Run Houston
description: A jingle-bell-themed holiday 5K at T.C. Jester Park in northwest Houston.

name: Conroe 10 Miler
description: A 10-mile road race in Conroe, north of Houston. Part of the Texas Bridge Series.

name: Kingwood Christmas Cookie Dash
description: The 3rd Annual Kingwood Christmas Cookie Dash on the trails of Kingwood Country Club, co-located with the Kingwood Christmas Market. Offers 5K, 10K, a unique 10K relay (4 legs of 1.5 miles each — runners decorate a cookie before tagging off), and Kids Dash. Benefiting Lily's Toy Box, Toys for Tots, and Angel Tree Houston. NOTE: The 2026 edition is already sold out.

name: Candy Cane Course - Houston
description: A running race in Sugar Land, TX.

name: Rudolph Fun Run
description: A Rudolph-themed holiday fun run at CityCentre in west Houston.

name: Reindeer Fun Run
description: A reindeer-themed holiday 5K in Nassau Bay, near the Clear Lake/NASA area.

name: Coach Andy Stewart's 30K & 10K
description: A 30K (18.6 miles) and 10K in Sugar Land, a key final long-run training race for Houston Marathon runners in January. Named for legendary Houston running coach Andy Stewart.

name: Houston's 12K of Christmas
description: Houston Running Co.'s 14th-annual holiday tradition at Sam Houston Park in downtown, offering a 12K and companion 6K through the city's historic core. The festive post-race party includes hot cocoa, Santa Claus appearances, Christmas carolers, and Karbach beer; also serves as the final race in the Houston Holiday Series.

name: Santa Hustle Galveston
description: A Christmas-costumed road race along Galveston's seawall starting at Beach Central, with a half marathon, 10K, 5K, and kids dash. Aid stations are stocked with candy canes and holiday treats; finishers receive a long-sleeve ugly-sweater dri-fit shirt, medal, and beanie.

name: Candy Cane Course
description: A candy-cane-themed holiday 5K in Sugar Land.

name: Houston Resolution Run
description: A year-end race at Fish Plaza in downtown Houston with flexible start times. Offers a Nearly Half Marathon, 10K, 5K, and Family 1 Mile. A popular way to close out the running year and set New Year's resolutions.

name: Texas Marathon Kingwood
description: A New Year's Day tradition since 2000, run entirely on the concrete greenbelt trails of Kingwood winding past Kingwood Lake. The marathon is four loops and the half marathon two loops on a USATF-certified course; capped at 700 runners and operated by the Knights of Columbus with proceeds supporting children's charities.

name: 48th Annual Lone Star Stampede
description: The 48th annual Texas Exes Houston Chapter scholarship run at Brazos River Park, featuring a 5K, 10K, kids dash, and — new for 2027 — a first-ever half marathon. A Houston tradition for UT Austin alumni and supporters, with proceeds funding college scholarships for Houston-area students.

name: We Are Houston 5K presented by Aramco and Chevron
description: The Saturday 5K kicking off the 2027 Chevron Houston Marathon weekend, starting and finishing near Minute Maid Park in downtown Houston. Runners who also complete Sunday's marathon or half marathon earn a triple-medal Houston Double challenge set.

name: Chevron Houston Marathon & Aramco Houston Half Marathon
description: The 55th Chevron Houston Marathon and 26th Aramco Houston Half Marathon, drawing over 27,000 participants to one of the fastest marathon courses in the world. The flat, single-loop course starts and finishes at Minute Maid Park near the George R. Brown Convention Center, qualifies for both Boston and the World Marathon Majors, and is historically run in January's ideal cool conditions.

name: SKECHERS Hot Chocolate Run Houston
description: America's sweetest road race starting and finishing at Sam Houston Park, with the course running along Allen Parkway beside Buffalo Bayou. Offered in 5K (7:15 AM), 10K, and 10-mile distances; every finisher receives a chocolate fondue mug with dippables, a commemorative full-zip SKECHERS jacket, and a finisher medal.

name: Run the Distance for Kylie - 5th Annual Event
description: A running race in Houston, TX.
